mechanism_type: intramolecular sulfamate ester formation followed by syn elimination
key_reagents_clue: Burgess reagent, sulfamate ester, mild heating, secondary or tertiary alcohol
common_conditions: mild dehydrating conditions; syn elimination; compatible with sensitive substrates
description_short: Mild dehydration of alcohols and related functional groups using the Burgess inner salt to give alkenes, nitriles, or related dehydrated products.

key_reagents_clue: concentrated NaOH, KOH, Lewis acid variants, aldehydes lacking alpha-hydrogen
common_solvents: water, alcohols, mixed aqueous basic media
common_conditions: strongly basic conditions; crossed or intramolecular variants possible
synonym_names: crossed Cannizzaro reaction; intramolecular Cannizzaro reaction
description_short: Disproportionation of non-enolizable aldehydes to alcohols and carboxylates through hydride transfer under basic conditions.

key_reagents_clue: allylic beta-keto esters, ester enolates, heat, LDA, decarboxylation
common_conditions: thermal or base-induced rearrangement of allylic beta-keto esters to gamma,delta-unsaturated ketones
description_short: Rearrangement of allylic beta-keto esters to gamma,delta-unsaturated ketones via a [3,3]-sigmatropic shift and loss of carbon dioxide.

mechanism_type: copper acetylide coupling with aryl or vinyl halides through a four-centered transition state
key_reagents_clue: CuI, copper acetylides, aryl/vinyl iodides or bromides, pyridine or basic media
common_conditions: copper-mediated alkynylation under reflux or elevated temperature
description_short: Copper-mediated coupling of aryl or vinyl halides with alkynes or copper acetylides to form substituted alkynes and related heterocycles.

transformation_type: heteroaromatic amination / nucleophilic substitution of hydride
mechanism_type: amide-ion-promoted nucleophilic aromatic substitution on electron-deficient azines with hydride loss
key_reagents_clue: NaNH2, KNH2, liquid ammonia, toluene, high temperature, azines or diazines
common_conditions: strongly basic amination of electron-deficient heteroarenes
description_short: Direct amination of electron-deficient nitrogen heteroarenes by amide equivalents through nucleophilic substitution of hydride.
